re_line: KOSPI -12.7% from June peak · Samsung Q2 record $58.5B profit · ROK-NATO procurement talks · Lee at NATO · SMR trilateral · Kim mausoleum visit
morning_memo: The KOSPI has dropped 20% from its June record close as chipmakers drag, prompting the ROK government to announce it will closely watch risks around stock market volatility.
Samsung reported a record $58.5 billion operating profit in Q2, surpassing Nvidia, driven by the semiconductor boom — even as chip stocks flip to losses on lingering AI and memory pricing concerns.

President Lee Jae-myung asked NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte on the sidelines of the Turkey summit to support halting North Korea's nuclear and missile development, and Seoul announced ROK-US-Japan agreement to cooperate on small modular reactor deployment.

on_this_day.event: North Korea fired a submarine-launched ballistic missile (SLBM), presumed to be a KN-11, from the east coast city of Sinpo.
on_this_day.relevance: Ten years ago, DPRK demonstrated an early SLBM capability from Sinpo — the same shipyard now hosting the Kang Kon destroyer weapons tests Kim Jong Un oversaw on July 5, 2026.

key_stat.label: ROK current account surplus in May 2026 — new record high
key_stat.context: Korea posted its highest-ever monthly current account surplus in May, driven by strong semiconductor exports, even as the KOSPI has fallen 20% from its June record close on chip valuation concerns.
key_stat.source: Hankyoreh English — Korea posts $38.61 billion current account surplus in May

bp_locations.name: Yongbyon Nuclear Complex
bp_locations.note: Apr 2026: Enrichment building expansion complete, new centrifuge hall operational. 5 MWe reactor and IRT-2000 both running. Thermal signatures consistent with increased plutonium/HEU production. AEI's June 2026 special report on DPRK constitutional amendments notes the nuclear program's posture is now formally entrenched in state law.

bp_locations.name: Sinpo South Shipyard
bp_locations.note: Jun 30, 2026: NK Pro satellite imagery shows the Choe Hyon destroyer, commissioned June 25 by Kim Jong Un, remains docked at a west coast civilian cargo port one week after KCNA claimed it departed for operations. NK Pro also uncovered previously unreported details about where a test of its sister ship (Kang Kon) was conducted on the east coast. Feb 2025: Hero Kim Kun Ok SLBM submarine exited drydock after 2+ years of fitting-out but still not operational as of May 2025. New submarine construction begun in separate bay.

bp_locations.name: THAAD Site — Seongju County
bp_locations.note: Jun 21, 2026: All six THAAD launchers returned to the permanent Seongju site from Osan Air Base, restoring the battery's full deployment footprint. Battery operational status remains subject to ongoing assessment after the partial Middle East redeployment.

bp_locations.name: Sohae Satellite Launch Station
bp_locations.note: May 2026: Rocket engine test conducted last week, possibly for military reconnaissance satellite program revival. New viewing area building nearing completion. Previous reports showed 2,500 kN engine test stand firing and facility expansion.

bp_locations.name: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: ROK DIA assessed Tunnel No. 3 is test-ready and a nuclear test can be conducted at any time upon political decision. Site maintained at high readiness since mid-2024.

bp_locations.name: Tumangang–Khasan (NK-Russia border)
bp_locations.note: Jul 1–3, 2026: 38 North assesses the opening of the new Tumen River vehicle bridge as unlikely in the near term; customs infrastructure remains incomplete on both sides despite the bridge nearing physical completion. Earlier reporting had flagged a June 19 scheduled opening. Passenger rail service resumed mid-2025; ore and tank car traffic at highest levels since project began.

bp_locations.name: Sinuiju–Dandong (NK-China border)
bp_locations.note: Mar 12, 2026: Cross-border passenger train service resumed between Sinuiju and Dandong after COVID-era suspension. New Yalu River Bridge may open in 2026. NK Pro satellite imagery analysis published July 7, 2026 shows over a dozen makeshift smuggling routes across the NK-China border that went quiet since late 2025 have appeared active again in recent weeks.

bp_locations.name: Rason SEZ
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: Chinese businesspeople re-entry to Rason SEZ expanded. Coal exports through Rason at highest levels in years throughout 2025. Arms export berth also active.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea NLL
bp_locations.note: Sep 26, 2025: 140m DPRK merchant vessel Toksong crossed NLL near Baengnyeong Island, advanced 5km south. ROK Navy fired ~60 warning shots; vessel retreated after 1 hour. Ship switched AIS nationality to China and flew Chinese flag. First NLL intrusion in three years.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea PMZ
bp_locations.note: Atlantic Amsterdam (converted oil rig, Chinese aquaculture platform) relocated out of PMZ Jan 27-28, 2026 to Weihai shipyard following Lee Jae-myung Beijing state visit. Two giant aquaculture cages (Shen Lan 1, Shen Lan 2) and 13 buoys remain in the PMZ.

bp_locations.name: Vostochny/Dunai (Russian Far East)
bp_locations.note: Jan 2026: Arms shipments slowed due to ice — one Russian vessel (likely Angara or Lady R) visited Rason, docked Jan 14 at arms export berth. Cargo routed to Vostochny. Since Sep 2023: 64 voyages by 4 vessels delivered ~15,800 containers from Rajin to Vostochny/Dunai.

rok_government.action: Lee requests NATO support for halting DPRK nuclear and missile development at Turkey summit sidelines
rok_government.detail: President Lee met with NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Turkey and stated that 'breaking the deadlock' on North Korea's weapons pursuit required NATO support. Lee also signaled interest in ROK-NATO cooperation in arms R&D and production.

rok_government.action: ROK, US, and Japan agree to cooperate on small modular reactor (SMR) deployment
rok_government.detail: Seoul announced that South Korea, the United States, and Japan have agreed to cooperate on SMR deployment, reached on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Turkey. The agreement adds a civilian nuclear energy dimension to the Camp David-era trilateral framework.

rok_government.action: ROK-NATO procurement talks to begin as Seoul eyes major defense export market
rok_government.detail: Korea and NATO are set to begin talks on a procurement deal, per Hankyoreh, as Seoul seeks to expand arms exports. The move follows ROK defense industry gains in Poland and NATO member states and President Lee's NATO summit attendance.

rok_government.action: PM Han identifies suicide prevention and lonely deaths as priority policy focus
rok_government.detail: Prime Minister Han Seong-sook stated during a visit to Ven. Jinwoo, chief of the Jogye Order, that reducing suicides and lonely deaths is one of her main focuses, in line with President Lee's 'politics saving people's lives' policy goal. Han said President Lee instructed the Cabinet to study various death-related policy factors after her appointment.

rok_government.official: Shin Young-kyu, Director General, consumer policy bureau
rok_government.action: KMCC designates Google, Meta, TikTok, Naver, Kakao under revised 'fake news' law
rok_government.detail: Korea's media regulator designated eight major online platforms — Google, Meta, TikTok, X, Naver, Kakao, Nate, and DC Inside — as subject to regulation under the revised Information and Communications Network Act, which took effect Tuesday. The law applies to services where users can post information others can view and share.

calendar_watch.headline: BOK Monetary Policy Board meeting — rate decision
calendar_watch.detail: The Bank of Korea's Monetary Policy Board meets for its July rate decision. The BOK rate currently stands at 2.50% following a July 2026 change; the KOSPI's 20% drop from June highs may factor into deliberations.

calendar_watch.headline: Section 122 surcharge expiry — 150-day statutory limit
calendar_watch.detail: The 10% global Section 122 surcharge expires on this date unless Congress acts to extend it. The USTR Section 301 investigation into South Korea and 15 other trading partners (launched Mar 11, 2026) could provide alternative legal authority for tariffs post-expiry.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean War armistice anniversary — 73rd
calendar_watch.detail: The 73rd anniversary of the July 27, 1953 armistice signing. DPRK has used this date historically for military demonstrations; KCNA escalation framing ahead of the date has been flagged in the July 7 bottom line.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean Liberation Day (광복절) — national holiday
calendar_watch.detail: Liberation Day is politically significant for presidential speeches on inter-Korean policy. President Lee Jae-myung's address will be closely watched for signals on engagement posture toward Pyongyang.

calendar_watch.headline: Democratic Party convention — new party leader elected
calendar_watch.detail: The DPK holds its national convention to elect a new leader and Supreme Council for a two-year term under the Lee Jae-myung administration. Candidates Rep. Song Young-gil, Rep. Koh Min-jung, former PM Kim Min-seok, and expected entrant Rep. Jung Chung-rae are competing.

rok_assembly.action: Democratic Party holds LJC session unilaterally
rok_assembly.detail: The Democratic Party of Korea held a National Assembly Legislation and Judiciary Committee session without opposition participation, per 매일경제 reporting. No further legislative detail was available from the source.

overnight_items.headline: Lee asks NATO's Rutte to support halting DPRK nuclear and missile development
overnight_items.body_text: President Lee Jae-myung met with NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Turkey and called for NATO support in breaking the deadlock on North Korea's weapons pursuit. Lee also expressed interest in ROK-NATO cooperation in arms R&D and production, per a presidential office statement.

overnight_items.headline: Korea posts $38.61B current account surplus in May — new record high
overnight_items.body_text: South Korea recorded a $38.61 billion current account surplus in May 2026, a new record high, driven by strong semiconductor exports. The record surplus arrives as the KOSPI has fallen sharply from its June highs, reflecting divergence between Korea's trade competitiveness and equity market sentiment on chip valuations.

overnight_items.headline: ROK Navy P-8A demonstrates submarine-hunting capability at RIMPAC for first time
overnight_items.body_text: The Republic of Korea Navy deployed its newly introduced P-8A Poseidon maritime patrol aircraft at RIMPAC 2026, marking the first time the aircraft participated in the exercise and the first time journalists observed an operational mission onboard a ROK Navy P-8A. The joint anti-submarine warfare mission with a U.S. Navy P-8A targeted a submarine simulator designed to replicate real submarine movements.

top_stories.headline: KOSPI drops 20% from June record close as chipmakers drag — government flags volatility watch
top_stories.body: The KOSPI has fallen 20% from its June record close, with chipmakers leading the decline amid concerns over AI demand sustainability and memory pricing, Reuters reported. Korean chip stocks flipped to losses despite Samsung posting a record $58.5 billion Q2 operating profit. The ROK government said it will closely monitor risks around market volatility.
top_stories.so_what: Directly affects BOK deliberations at the July 10 Monetary Policy Board meeting.

top_stories.headline: ROK, US, Japan agree to cooperate on SMR deployment; Lee eyes NATO arms R&D partnership
top_stories.body: South Korea, the United States, and Japan agreed to cooperate on small modular reactor deployment on the sidelines of the NATO summit in Turkey, Seoul announced. President Lee Jae-myung separately met NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte to request NATO support for stopping North Korea's nuclear and missile development and flagged interest in ROK-NATO arms R&D and production cooperation.
top_stories.so_what: Sets agenda for the August Ulchi Freedom Shield exercise period and upcoming trilateral diplomatic engagements.

top_stories.headline: Samsung posts record $58.5B Q2 operating profit, surpassing Nvidia
top_stories.body: Samsung Electronics reported a record $58.5 billion operating profit in Q2 2026, exceeding Nvidia's quarterly result, per the Korea Economic Daily. The result is driven by the global semiconductor boom even as chip stocks fell on valuation concerns.
top_stories.so_what: Informs ROK semiconductor tax windfall debate and the education funding formula review currently before the National Assembly.

also_today.headline: Kim Jong Un visits Kumsusan Palace at midnight for Kim Il Sung's 32nd death anniversary
also_today.body_text: Kim Jong Un led dozens of officials in black ties to the Kumsusan Palace of the Sun at midnight on July 8 to mark the 32nd anniversary of Kim Il Sung's death, per KCNA. The midnight ritual timing and formal political messaging reinforce the personality cult and regime legitimacy framing.

also_today.headline: NK News podcast: DPRK naval modernization, China border trade revival, and Seoul's coexistence dilemma
also_today.body_text: NK News Senior Analytic Correspondent Colin Zwirko discusses North Korea's rapid naval buildup — including the Kang Kon destroyer cruise missile tests and the Choe Hyon commissioning — alongside renewed NK-China border smuggling activity and the policy dilemma Seoul faces on coexistence versus pressure.

also_today.headline: Military academy alumni groups rally at National Assembly against Lee government's unification plan
also_today.body_text: Alumni associations of the Army, Navy, and Air Force academies rallied at the National Assembly, calling on the Lee Jae-myung government to halt its plan to merge the three academies into a single National Military Academy. The government's push is framed around demographic decline and the need to pool talented future officer candidates.

also_today.headline: DPK leadership race gains fourth entrant ahead of Aug 17 convention
also_today.body_text: Rep. Koh Min-jung entered the Democratic Party of Korea leadership race, joining Rep. Song Young-gil, former PM Kim Min-seok, and expected entrant Rep. Jung Chung-rae ahead of the August 17 convention. Candidates are centering platforms on closer alignment with President Lee and youth voter outreach.

also_today.headline: South Korea's chip hub plans face tough questions over timing and demand
also_today.body_text: Nikkei Asia examines South Korea's semiconductor cluster plans, raising questions about whether domestic and global demand can absorb the scale of planned capacity expansion and whether the timing is right given current market volatility. The KOSPI's 20% decline from its June high reflects similar investor uncertainty.

also_today.headline: Paradoxical polarization fueled by Korea's semiconductor windfall
also_today.body_text: Hankyoreh examines how South Korea's semiconductor boom is generating record corporate tax revenues that are automatically channeling into education budgets under a decades-old formula, even as the school-age population shrinks — creating political friction over redistribution. The education allocation has more than doubled over the past decade to a projected record 80 trillion won this year.

investment_package.latest_update: No new US-Korea investment deals announced today; tracker reflects the National Assembly's passage of the Special Investment Act (Mar 12, 2026) establishing the Korea-US Strategic Investment Corporation to implement the $350B commitment.

known_deals.company: Samsung Heavy Industries / Vigor Marine Group

trade_policy.item: Section 301 Investigation — South Korea and 15 other trading partners
trade_policy.detail: Launched Mar 11, 2026; could provide new legal authority for tariffs after Section 122 surcharge expires Jul 24, 2026.

trade_policy.item: BIS Semiconductor Export Controls — Samsung and SK Hynix China fabs
trade_policy.detail: Annual BIS export licenses renewed Dec 30, 2025 for 2026; maintenance/incremental upgrades only, no capacity expansion permitted at China fabs.
Foreign Entity of Concern rules affect LG Energy Solution, SK On, Samsung SDI on China-sourced materials; battery components phase-in Dec 2024, critical minerals Dec 2025.

trade_policy.item: CHIPS Act Guardrails — Samsung Taylor TX fabs
trade_policy.detail: Samsung preliminary $6.4B CHIPS award (Apr 2024) for Taylor TX fabs carries 10-year restriction on materially expanding semiconductor manufacturing in countries of concern; Samsung Xi'an NAND fab limited to legacy production.

tariff_tracker.headline_note: A 10% Section 122 surcharge applies to all Korean goods effective Feb 24, 2026, set to expire Jul 24, 2026 unless Congress extends; the US-Korea Strategic Trade and Investment Deal framework (Jul 30, 2025) set a 15% IEEPA rate that was struck down by the Supreme Court Feb 20, 2026.

sector_rates.note: All country exemptions/TRQs eliminated Mar 2025; no exemption under US-Korea deal.
Reduced from 25% under US-Korea deal, retroactive to Nov 1, 2025 per USTR Dec 3, 2025 implementation notice.
Reduced under US-Korea deal per USTR Dec 3, 2025 implementation notice.
Effective Jan 15, 2026; broader semiconductor tariffs subject to Commerce/USTR report; deal promises Korea terms no less favorable than comparable partners.
Rate cap agreed in US-Korea deal but specific Section 232 action not yet issued.
Tariffs removed per USTR Dec 3, 2025 notice.

sector_rates.sector: Generic Pharma / Precursors / Rare Natural Resources

tariff_tracker.section_122_surcharge: 10% global surcharge (Section 122, effective Feb 24 2026, expires Jul 24 2026)
tariff_tracker.last_change: Feb 24, 2026 — 10% Section 122 surcharge imposed on all countries following Supreme Court Feb 20 IEEPA ruling (6-3)
tariff_tracker.next_trigger: Jul 24, 2026 — Section 122 surcharge expiry (150-day statutory limit); Section 301 investigations ongoing (launched Mar 11, 2026)

business_economy.headline: South Korean auto parts makers pivot to robotics to capture $370B market
business_economy.body_text: South Korean auto parts manufacturers are shifting production lines toward robotics to compete for a projected $370 billion global robotics market, per the Korea Economic Daily. The pivot responds to declining internal combustion engine demand and aligns with the Hyundai Motor Group's robotics hub investment in the US under the $350B commitment.

business_economy.headline: LG Energy shares drop after Q2 operating profit misses consensus
business_economy.body_text: LG Energy Solution shares fell after the company reported Q2 operating profit below market consensus expectations. The miss adds pressure to South Korea's battery sector amid the broader KOSPI selloff driven by chipmaker weakness.

business_economy.headline: Semiconductor tax boom pushes education allocation to record 80 trillion won, reviving reform debate
business_economy.body_text: Korea's 20.79% domestic tax revenue earmark for education is set to channel a record 80 trillion won ($53B) to schools in 2026, nearly double the 2015 allocation, even as the school-age population shrinks from 6.4 million to 4.92 million. Fiscal authorities and policymakers are pressing to overhaul the formula at a public forum co-hosted this week.

northeast_asia.headline: Korea and NATO to begin talks on procurement deal as Seoul eyes defense export market
northeast_asia.body_text: South Korea and NATO are set to begin procurement talks, with Seoul targeting the NATO defense market for its arms industry following export successes in Poland and other member states. President Lee Jae-myung made the case for ROK-NATO arms R&D and production cooperation at the Turkey summit.

northeast_asia.headline: 1948 US military documents identify Dokdo as Korean territory — Dong-A Ilbo and Hankyoreh
northeast_asia.body_text: Declassified 1948 US military files identify Dokdo as Korean territory, according to both Dong-A Ilbo and Hankyoreh, providing new documentary evidence in the longstanding Japan-Korea dispute over the islets. The documents predate the 1952 Rhee Line and the 1951 San Francisco Peace Treaty, which are central to Japan's competing claim.

northeast_asia.headline: DPRK state media says Japan overseas aggression is 'reality, not hypothetical'
northeast_asia.body_text: North Korean state media published commentary declaring that Japan's overseas aggression is a 'reality, not hypothetical,' the sharpest DPRK rhetorical escalation toward Tokyo in recent weeks, per Japan Today citing KCNA. The statement coincides with ongoing North Korean naval modernization and Kim Jong Un's commissioning of two destroyers in less than two weeks.

gallup_spotlight.finding: 67% of respondents assessed election administration as failed in the ballot shortage incident; 25% said it constituted evidence of election fraud — Gallup Korea June 2nd week survey.

rok_personnel.name: Han Seong-sook
rok_personnel.detail: PM Han Seong-sook is confirmed as Prime Minister under President Lee Jae-myung, per Korea Times reporting on her stated policy priorities including suicide prevention. She referenced President Lee's instruction at her first Cabinet meeting.

opeds_today.headline: Dispelling the misconceptions surrounding North Korea's DMZ fortifications
opeds_today.central_argument: North Korea's DMZ refortification does not automatically constitute a violation of the 1953 armistice agreement, and several widespread characterizations of these actions are inaccurate.
opeds_today.summary: The piece examines the legal and military dimensions of DPRK DMZ refortification, addressing whether specific actions violate the armistice. It also notes friction between the ROK government and the US-led UN Command over interpretation of these activities. The analysis distinguishes hostile intent from technical armistice violations.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Directly relevant to ROK-UNC coordination on armistice enforcement as the July 27 armistice anniversary approaches and DPRK military posture intensifies.

opeds_today.headline: Korean Peninsula Update, July 7, 2026
opeds_today.central_argument: The DPRK's constitutional entrenchment of nuclear status, rapid naval modernization, and continued weapons testing reflect an accelerating military posture that Seoul and Washington must address before the armistice anniversary.
opeds_today.summary: AEI's weekly Korean Peninsula Update synthesizes developments including DPRK naval activity, Kim Jong Un's weapons oversight appearances, and the broader strategic posture. The update contextualizes the Kang Kon destroyer tests and Choe Hyon commissioning within the June 25 Workers' Party plenum directives. It also notes the constitutional formalization of DPRK nuclear status.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Informs extended deterrence planning ahead of the August Ulchi Freedom Shield exercise and the July 27 armistice anniversary.

opeds_today.headline: Export Nation or Ecosystem Power: South Korea's Choice in the AI Industrial Age
opeds_today.central_argument: South Korea must choose between remaining a semiconductor and technology export platform or building a full AI industrial ecosystem domestically — and the current policy trajectory risks locking it into the former.
opeds_today.summary: The CSIS piece frames a structural choice facing Seoul as the semiconductor windfall generates record revenues: whether to invest in upstream AI ecosystem development or continue to rely on hardware exports. It connects the chip boom to broader industrial policy questions around talent, capital allocation, and supply chain positioning. The analysis is directly relevant to debates over the semiconductor tax windfall and education funding formula.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Informs the Lee administration's AI and industrial policy agenda and the National Assembly debate on semiconductor tax revenue allocation.

opeds_today.headline: Why Kim Jong Un is paranoid about South Korean media in his country
opeds_today.central_argument: Kim Jong Un has systematically expanded repressive legislation against South Korean media infiltration since 2011 because foreign content directly threatens the ideological control underpinning his rule.
opeds_today.summary: The piece traces Kim Jong Un's escalating campaign against South Korean media inside North Korea from 2011 to the present, including the expansion of laws criminalizing exposure to foreign content. It connects the legislative crackdown to the regime's assessment that ROK cultural influence poses an existential political threat. The analysis contextualizes the personality cult expansion — including large Kim murals now being installed in city centers — as a parallel defensive response.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Relevant to ROK MOU and US-funded information operations targeting DPRK audiences, including balloon leaflet programs and USB distribution networks.

opeds_today.headline: North Korea Policy Symposium — How to Overcome North Korea's Food Crisis: How to Carry Out Effective Economic Support to North Korea
opeds_today.central_argument: Effective humanitarian and economic support to North Korea requires a new policy framework that bypasses current sanctions constraints while maintaining pressure on Pyongyang's weapons programs.
opeds_today.summary: The Sejong Institute symposium summary examines pathways for food and economic assistance to North Korea given the severity of DPRK food insecurity and the limits of the current sanctions regime. It addresses operational and political constraints facing South Korean and international humanitarian actors. The piece is relevant to ongoing ROK MOU inter-Korean engagement policy debates under the Lee administration.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Informs ROK MOU's humanitarian engagement posture and the Lee administration's approach to resuming inter-Korean cooperation.

academic_today.headline: The heads of foreign affairs of South Korea, the United States and Japan will gather together to discuss...
academic_today.summary: This item references the gathering of the foreign ministers of South Korea, the United States, and Japan to discuss shared security and policy issues, per 매일경제 sourcing a Foreign Affairs-tier outlet. The trilateral foreign ministers' meeting is a key forum for coordinating positions on DPRK denuclearization, extended deterrence, and regional stability. The meeting agenda likely includes North Korea's accelerating naval and missile programs and the post-July 24 tariff architecture.
academic_today.policy_implication: Outputs from the trilateral FM meeting will shape the diplomatic posture heading into the July 27 armistice anniversary and the August Ulchi Freedom Shield exercise.
academic_today.bp_link: Relevant to CSIS Korea Chair NK-Russia-China axis tracking and ROK-US-Japan trilateral coordination database.

kcna_delta.kim_activity: Kim Jong Un visited the Kumsusan Palace of the Sun at midnight on July 8 to mark the 32nd anniversary of Kim Il Sung's death, leading dozens of officials in black ties in a ceremonial tribute.

senior_officials.name: Unknown
senior_officials.activity: North Korean state media published commentary declaring Japan's overseas aggression is a 'reality, not hypothetical,' a direct escalation in anti-Japan rhetoric accompanying the DPRK naval modernization push.

kcna_delta.bottom_line: KCNA's lead outputs today are Kim Jong Un's midnight mausoleum visit for the 32nd Kim Il Sung death anniversary and continued escalatory rhetoric targeting Japan as an aggressor state. Watch for further DPRK escalation framing as the July 27 armistice anniversary approaches — now 19 days out.
